summary: wallet-security-checker exfiltrates host and wallet data to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages C2
details: Package name advertises a wallet/security utility but ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine OS/host enumeration, filesystem inspection, child_process command execution, and outbound HTTPS to a hardcoded attacker endpoint. scanner.js line 161/323 hits https://ddjidd564.github.io — a personal GitHub Pages account with no relation to any wallet-security publisher — using https.get/https.request and POST with payloads carrying os.hostname(), username, cwd and `whoami`/curl/ping output. wallet-scanner.js performs additional POSTs and curl-based exfiltration (lines 723/795/871, 1204/1229/1314). build-verifier.js compounds the picture with execSync('docker push...') alongside its own POST/https.request flow. The combination of (a) hardcoded non-publisher C2 (ddjidd564.github.io), (b) host/identity enumeration via os.hostname/username/cwd shipped to that endpoint, and (c) shell-execution primitives in the same code path is the canonical credential/host stealer fingerprint. Any developer running this package — given its name, likely on a machine that holds wallet keys or wallet-adjacent secrets — has their host fingerprint and command output sent to an attacker-controlled host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
